description: Given an array of strings, group the anagrams together. You may return the answer in any order; the order of the groups and the order of words within a group do not matter. Input: first line n, then n whitespace-separated words. Output: each group on its own line, words separated by a space.
notes: The partition into anagram groups is unique; only listing order is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given an array of distinct integers and a target, return every unordered pair {a,b} of array values with a+b=target. Pairs may be listed in any order and a pair may be printed in either order. Input: first line n and target, then n integers. Output: one pair per line.
notes: The set of qualifying pairs is fixed; only ordering of the listing is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given an integer n >= 2, return its prime factors with multiplicity, in any order. Input: a single integer n. Output: the prime factors separated by spaces.
notes: The multiset of prime factors is unique; only order is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given a string, return all of its distinct permutations, in any order. Input: a single string. Output: each permutation on its own line.
notes: The set of permutations is fixed; only the listing order is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given a set of distinct integers, return all of its subsets. Subsets may appear in any order, and elements within a subset in any order. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: each subset on its own line (empty line for the empty subset).
notes: The collection of subsets is fixed; only ordering is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given an undirected graph, return its connected components, each as the list of its vertices. Components may be listed in any order and vertices within a component in any order. Input: first line V and E, then E edges. Output: one component per line.
notes: The partition into components is unique; only ordering of vertices/components is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given an array of integers, return all values that appear more than once, each value once, in any order. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the duplicate values separated by spaces.
notes: The set of duplicated values is fixed; only order is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given a positive integer n, return all of its divisors, in any order. Input: a single integer n. Output: the divisors separated by spaces.
notes: The set of divisors is fixed; only order is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given a list of words, return each distinct word together with how many times it appears. The word/count pairs may be printed in any order. Input: first line n, then n words. Output: 'word count' per line.
notes: The set of word->count pairs is fixed; only listing order is free -> ANY_ORDER.

description: Given a DAG with a source s and a target t, return ALL distinct paths from s to t, each as a vertex sequence. The paths may be listed in any order. Input: first line V, E, s, t, then E directed edges. Output: one path per line.
notes: Each path is a fixed ordered sequence and the full SET of paths is fixed; only the order in which the (complete) set of paths is listed is free -> ANY_ORDER. Contrast with returning ANY single path (ANY_VALID).

description: Given beginWord, endWord, and a dictionary wordList, return ALL the shortest transformation sequences from beginWord to endWord, where each adjacent pair of words differs by a single letter and every intermediate word is in wordList. Each sequence is the list of words [beginWord, ..., endWord]. Return an empty list if none exist. Input: beginWord, endWord, then n and n dictionary words. Output: one sequence per line (words space-separated).
notes: Word Ladder II. 'Return ALL shortest sequences' -> the complete set of sequences is uniquely determined; only the order in which the list of sequences is printed varies (each sequence's own order is fixed) -> ANY_ORDER, resolved by sorting the outer list. It is NOT ANY_VALID and needs NO validator. Trap the agent previously failed: it picked a recursive validator -> StackOverflow.

description: Given distinct positive integers, return the largest subset such that every pair (a,b) satisfies a%b==0 or b%a==0. If several subsets of the maximum size exist, return any one of them. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the subset elements separated by spaces.
notes: Two correct submissions can return genuinely different element sets of the same optimal size -> ANY_VALID.

description: Given a DAG, return any valid topological ordering of its vertices. Input: first line V and E, then E directed edges. Output: the vertices in a valid topological order, space-separated.
notes: The output is an ordered sequence; different valid orderings are genuinely different sequences (not a reordering of an unordered collection) -> ANY_VALID. Common trap that looks like ANY_ORDER.

description: Given an undirected graph with vertices s and t, return ANY one simple path from s to t (the vertices in order). If no path exists, return an empty line. Input: first line V, E, s, t, then E edges. Output: the path vertices space-separated.
notes: Different valid paths use different vertices -> the answer elements can differ -> ANY_VALID.

description: Given an array, return ANY one longest strictly increasing subsequence (the elements in order). If several have the maximum length, return any one. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the subsequence elements space-separated.
notes: Several LIS of equal length can have different elements -> ANY_VALID. Contrast with returning only the LENGTH (SINGLE).

description: Given n, place n queens on an n x n board so that none attack each other and return ANY one valid placement as the column index chosen in each row. Input: a single integer n. Output: n column indices (one per row), space-separated.
notes: Many distinct valid placements exist -> ANY_VALID.

description: Given an array of positive integers whose total is even, partition it into two subsets with equal sum and return the elements of ONE of the two subsets (any valid choice). If impossible, print IMPOSSIBLE. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the chosen subset's elements space-separated.
notes: Different valid partitions yield different element sets -> ANY_VALID.

description: Given coin denominations and an amount, return ANY combination of coins (with repetition) of MINIMUM count that sums to the amount. If impossible, print -1. Input: first line k and amount, then k denominations. Output: the chosen coins space-separated.
notes: Several minimum-count combinations can use different coins -> ANY_VALID (optimality is fixed, the elements are not).

description: Given an array and a target, return the indices (i, j) of two elements that sum to the target. If multiple such pairs exist, return any one. Input: first line n and target, then n integers. Output: the two indices space-separated.
notes: Multiple index pairs can be valid and they differ in content -> ANY_VALID. Contrast with the 'exactly one solution' variant (SINGLE).

description: Given a string and a dictionary of words, return ANY one way to segment the string into a sequence of dictionary words. If impossible, print IMPOSSIBLE. Input: first line the string, second line the dictionary words. Output: the chosen words space-separated.
notes: Different valid segmentations differ in content -> ANY_VALID.

description: Given a connected undirected unweighted graph, return the edges of ANY spanning tree. Input: first line V and E, then E edges. Output: V-1 edges, one 'u v' per line.
notes: Many distinct spanning trees exist; the chosen edge set can differ -> ANY_VALID.

description: Given an array of n integers, return their sum. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the sum.
notes: Unique answer -> SINGLE; the autograder already works.

description: Given an array of integers, return the maximum element. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the maximum value.
notes: Unique answer -> SINGLE.

description: Given an array of integers, return them sorted in non-decreasing order. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the sorted values space-separated.
notes: The sorted sequence is unique even with duplicates -> SINGLE. Trap: 'order' wording does not make it ANY_ORDER because the output is fully determined.

description: Given an array of integers, return how many distinct values it contains. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the count.
notes: Unique answer -> SINGLE.

description: Given an array, return the LENGTH of its longest strictly increasing subsequence. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the length.
notes: Only the length is asked; it is unique -> SINGLE. Trap vs returning the subsequence itself (ANY_VALID).

description: Given a grid of 0s and 1s, return the number of connected islands of 1s (4-directional). Input: first line R and C, then R rows. Output: the island count.
notes: A count is unique -> SINGLE.

description: Given a string, return YES if it is a palindrome and NO otherwise. Input: a single string. Output: YES or NO.
notes: Boolean answer is unique -> SINGLE.

description: Given two positive integers a and b, return their greatest common divisor. Input: two integers on one line. Output: the gcd.
notes: Unique answer -> SINGLE.

description: Given an array and a target, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target. Each input has exactly one solution and you may not use the same element twice. Input: first line n and target, then n integers. Output: the two indices space-separated.
notes: The problem GUARANTEES exactly one solution -> SINGLE. Trap: looks like two_sum_any_pair but the uniqueness guarantee makes it single-output.

description: Sort the given integers in non-decreasing order. Because equal values are indistinguishable, their relative order does not matter. Input: first line n, then n integers. Output: the sorted values space-separated.
notes: Despite the 'order does not matter' phrasing, the printed output is identical for all correct submissions -> SINGLE. Trap that should NOT be classified ANY_ORDER.
